stem: A scatterplot shows the density of different concentrations of orange juice, where the x-axis represents concentration (in percent) and the y-axis represents density (in kilograms per cubic meter). The data show that density increases linearly with concentration, passing through (10%, 1,020), (40%, 1,090), and (70%, 1,160). According to the trend shown by the data, which of the following is closest to the predicted density, in kilograms per cubic meter (kg/m³), for orange juice with a concentration of 55%?
rationale:
Choice B is correct. The slope is (1,090 − 1,020)/(40 − 10) ≈ 2.33 per percent. At concentration 55%, density ≈ 1,020 + 2.33·(55 − 10) = 1,020 + 105 = 1,125 kg/m³.

Other choices result from misreading the trend.
